heading: Bringing down the cost of health care coverage
label: 300gg–18 Bringing down the cost of health care coverage
notes: Amendments2010—, amended section generally. Prior to amendment, the section related to clear accounting for costs, ensuring that consumers receive value for premiums, standard hospital charges, and definitions.Pub. L. 111–148, § 10101(f)

Effective DateSection effective for plan years beginning on or after the date that is 6 months after , see , set out as a note under .Mar. 23, 2010section 1004 of Pub. L. 111–148section 300gg–11 of this title
amendments: Amendments2010—, amended section generally. Prior to amendment, the section related to clear accounting for costs, ensuring that consumers receive value for premiums, standard hospital charges, and definitions.Pub. L. 111–148, § 10101(f)

children.text: on reimbursement for clinical services provided to enrollees under such coverage;
for activities that improve health care quality; and
on all other non-claims costs, including an explanation of the nature of such costs, and excluding Federal and State taxes and licensing or regulatory fees.

children.heading: Ensuring that consumers receive value for their premium payments
children.label: b Ensuring that consumers receive value for their premium payments

children.heading: Requirement to provide value for premium payments
children.label: 1 Requirement to provide value for premium payments
i
children.text: with respect to a health insurance issuer offering coverage in the large group market, 85 percent, or such higher percentage as a State may by regulation determine; or

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to a health insurance issuer offering coverage in the small group market or in the individual market, 80 percent, or such higher percentage as a State may by regulation determine, except that the Secretary may adjust such percentage with respect to a State if the Secretary determines that the application of such 80 percent may destabilize the individual market in such State.

children.label: I
children.text: the amount by which the percentage described in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (A) exceeds the ratio described in such subparagraph; and

children.label: II
children.text: the total amount of premium revenue (excluding Federal and State taxes and licensing or regulatory fees and after accounting for payments or receipts for risk adjustment, risk corridors, and reinsurance under sections 18061, 18062, and 18063 of this title) for such plan year.

children.heading: Calculation based on average ratio
children.label: ii Calculation based on average ratio
children.text: Beginning on , the determination made under subparagraph (A) for the year involved shall be based on the averages of the premiums expended on the costs described in such subparagraph and total premium revenue for each of the previous 3 years for the plan.January 1, 2014

children.heading: Consideration in setting percentages
children.label: 2 Consideration in setting percentages
children.text: In determining the percentages under paragraph (1), a State shall seek to ensure adequate participation by health insurance issuers, competition in the health insurance market in the State, and value for consumers so that premiums are used for clinical services and quality improvements.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 3 Enforcement
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate regulations for enforcing the provisions of this section and may provide for appropriate penalties.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: c Definitions
children.text: Not later than , and subject to the certification of the Secretary, the National Association of Insurance Commissioners shall establish uniform definitions of the activities reported under subsection (a) and standardized methodologies for calculating measures of such activities, including definitions of which activities, and in what regard such activities, constitute activities described in subsection (a)(2). Such methodologies shall be designed to take into account the special circumstances of smaller plans, different types of plans, and newer plans.December 31, 2010

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: d Adjustments
children.text: The Secretary may adjust the rates described in subsection (b) if the Secretary determines appropriate on account of the volatility of the individual market due to the establishment of State Exchanges.

children.heading: Standard hospital charges
children.label: e Standard hospital charges
children.text: Each hospital operating within the United States shall for each year establish (and update) and make public (in accordance with guidelines developed by the Secretary) a list of the hospital’s standard charges for items and services provided by the hospital, including for diagnosis-related groups established under .section 1395ww(d)(4) of this title
